metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = fast / optical end of the spectrum, CRIMSON = slow / radio end of the spectrum
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no density-of-states derivation, no A-coefficient integral, no Einstein B-coefficient thermodynamic argument, no stimulated emission / laser connection
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B16: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. A hydrogen atom in empty space. One nanosecond later it has emitted a photon and fallen to its ground state — driven by nothing macroscopic.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. A hydrogen atom in empty space. One nanosecond later it has emitted a photon and fallen to its ground state — driven by nothing macro

beats.narration_text: A proton in a ten-tesla magnet sits in its excited spin state. Thirty seconds pass. It is still there. Same empty space. Same electromagnetic vacuum.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A proton in a ten-tesla magnet sits in its excited spin state. Thirty seconds pass. It is still there. Same empty space. Same electromagnetic vacuum.

beats.narration_text: Both couple to the same vacuum. The atom emits a billion times faster. Why does frequency alone change the rate by ten billion?

props.headline: Both couple to the same vacuum. The atom emits a billion…

card.headline: Same vacuum. Same physics. 10^9 times different.
card.dek: Why does the NMR proton emit 10^9 times slower than the optical atom?

beats.narration_text: Both transitions radiate into the same vacuum. Both involve the same kind of coupling: a quantum system emitting one photon. The vacuum is the same.

props.headline: Both transitions radiate into the same vacuum. Both involve the same…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Both transitions radiate into the same vacuum. Both involve the same kind of coupling: a quantum system emitting one photon. The vacuum is the same.

beats.narration_text: The only difference is frequency. The atom's photon is optical — six hundred terahertz. The proton's photon is radio — four hundred megahertz. A factor of a million and a half in frequency.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The only difference is frequency. The atom's photon is optical — six hundred terahertz. The proton's photon is radio — four hundred megahertz. A factor of a mil

beats.narration_text: A factor of a million in frequency should give a factor of a million in rate. It gives ten billion. Something makes the rate grow much faster than frequency alone.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A factor of a million in frequency should give a factor of a million in rate. It gives ten billion. Something makes the rate grow much faster than frequency alo

beats.narration_text: The spontaneous emission rate comes from Fermi's golden rule: it is proportional to the matrix element squared, times the density of final states.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The spontaneous emission rate comes from Fermi's golden rule: it is proportional to the matrix element squared, times the density of final states.

beats.narration_text: The density of photon states grows as omega squared — more available modes at higher frequencies. And the dipole matrix element converts the electric field to a rate with one more power of omega.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The density of photon states grows as omega squared — more available modes at higher frequencies. And the dipole matrix element converts the electric field to a

beats.narration_text: Together, the rate scales as omega cubed. Not linear in frequency — cubic. Every time the frequency doubles, the rate goes up by a factor of eight.

props.headline: Together, the rate scales as omega cubed. Not linear in frequency…

card.headline: Rate proportional to omega^3
card.dek: Density of states (omega^2) times one power of omega from the matrix element.

beats.narration_text: Plot the rate against frequency on a log scale. The cubic curve is nearly flat at radio frequencies and rockets upward at optical. The NMR proton sits at the bottom. The hydrogen atom sits at the top.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Plot the rate against frequency on a log scale. The cubic curve is nearly flat at radio frequencies and rockets upward at optical. The NMR proton sits at the bo

beats.narration_text: This is why NMR spin states are stable enough to use as qubits and to measure nuclear structure. Their long lifetime is not an accident — it is omega cubed at radio frequencies.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is why NMR spin states are stable enough to use as qubits and to measure nuclear structure. Their long lifetime is not an accident — it is omega cubed at r

beats.narration_text: And it explains why optical laser transitions can invert: the excited state lives long enough to pump population before it drains away spontaneously.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: And it explains why optical laser transitions can invert: the excited state lives long enough to pump population before it drains away spontaneously.

beats.narration_text: Illustrative: a hypothetical transition at 300 megahertz. Apply the Einstein A-coefficient formula with a dipole moment equal to the Bohr radius. The rate comes out three times ten to the minus seventeen per second — a lifetime of a billion years. The same formula at six hundred terahertz gives a rate of ten to the eighth — a ten-nanosecond lifetime.

beats.narration_text: Spontaneous emission slows to nearly zero at radio frequencies because the rate scales as the cube of frequency. Quantum mechanics.

props.headline: Spontaneous emission slows to nearly zero at radio frequencies because the…

card.headline: Rate scales as omega^3.
card.dek: Radio is not just slow — it is exponentially slow. Omega cubed at 10^8 vs. 10^15.
